samples.title: Sanké mon, collective fishing rite of the Sanké
samples.description:
Les mesures de sauvegarde mises à jour, susceptibles d’améliorer la viabilité du Sanké mon s’étendent sur quatre (4) ans. 
L’ensemble des activités qui seront menées dans le cadre des mesures de sauvegarde vise à sauvegarder le Sanké mon. Ainsi, les actions décrites, ci-dessous, comptent garantir la

samples.title: Settlement patterns: Segmented communities, i.e., those divided into barrios, wards, or hamlets, each of which is essentially a localized kin group, a clan or ramage, in the absence of any indication of local exogamy. Large extended families (see "Domestic organization"), are treated as clan-barrios if they are integrated by a rule of ambilineal, matrilineal, or patrilineal descent.

data_sources.name: D-PLACE (Database of Places, Language, Culture and Environment)
data_sources.ethical_notes: Academic ethnographic data. Compiled from published sources with varying consent histories.

data_sources.name: UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage
data_sources.ethical_notes: Fully public. All elements inscribed with community consent (FPIC compliant). Safest source.

data_sources.name: Standard Cross-Cultural Sample (SCCS)
data_sources.ethical_notes: Academic data from Murdock & White (1969). 186 representative societies, ~2,000 variables.

data_sources.name: Binford Hunter-Gatherer Dataset
data_sources.ethical_notes: Compiled by Lewis Binford from ethnographic literature. Focus on subsistence ecology.

data_sources.name: Ethnographic Atlas
data_sources.ethical_notes: George P. Murdock (1967). 1,291 societies. Integrated into D-PLACE.
